name: Huo Xing
page_title: Huo Xing 霍性 [Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes:
Huo Xing served as General of the Household to the court of Wei in AD 220 when Cao Pi prepared an inaugural tour as a display of strength to the south. Huo Xing memorialized against the tour, claiming it was inappropriate. Angered by the interference, Cao Pi ordered Huo Xing tortured and executed. It is claimed that he wished to rescind his orders, but not in time to spare Huo Xing’s life.

Source: de Crespigny, Rafe; Bibliographical History of the Three Kingdoms

full_text:
Huo Xing 霍性
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Huo Xing (霍性). Petitioned against Cao Pi’s inspection of the south. Tortured and executed before Pi changed his mind. Huo Xing was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom. Huo Xing lived during the Later Han Dynasty and Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Huo Xing served as General of the Household to the court of Wei in AD 220 when Cao Pi prepared an inaugural tour as a display of strength to the south. Huo Xing memorialized against the tour, claiming it was inappropriate. Angered by the interference, Cao Pi ordered Huo Xing tortured and executed. It is claimed that he wished to rescind his orders, but not in time to spare Huo Xing’s life.
Source: de Crespigny, Rafe;Bibliographical History of the Three Kingdoms
